messages.author: Steve ● 7acc
messages.text: I think most of the country is on the UK narrow setting. Every time I've been down that way there are a fair few meshtastic nodes (no messages ever received!), not seen any meshcore yet. It's probably a stretch but if you can get anything towards the Severn estuary/Bristol way there's a very active mesh.

messages.author: M0WIV
messages.text: Just joined. I'm based near Plympton a little east of Plymouth. I've configured a repeater and a couple of clients and got them talking to each other locally. What I don't have is an external antenna but that will arrive next week and I'll connect that to the repeater. Settings are UK Narrow. I'm not expecting much traffic as Meshcore nodes seem a bit scarce down here although there are a few in Cornwall. John

messages.author: S7uart G5AIL
messages.text: Just a heads-up – I’ve been tinkering with something called MeshRank It’s basically a way of giving a bit more visibility into what the mesh is actually doing, without changing how MeshCORE works. No internet routing, no messing with RF, nothing in the message path. It just listens, observes, and feeds back what already happened. Why I built it: • New repeaters often get switched off because it’s hard to tell if they’re helping • People end up spamming test messages just to see if anything’s alive • When stuff doesn’t propagate, there’s no clue why MeshRank gives things like delivery confidence, shows which repeaters are contributing, and highlights where messages are being seen but not going any further. Observers are totally passive — they don’t transmit or interfere in any way. If MeshRank fell over tomorrow, the mesh would carry on exactly the same. I’m looking for a few folks (UK first) who wouldn’t mind running an observer and helping build some early coverage — high ground / urban spots especially useful. If you fancy a look: https://meshrank.net Happy to answer questions or take criticism
messages.content_html: Just a heads-up – I’ve been tinkering with something called MeshRank It’s basically a way of giving a bit more visibility into what the mesh is actually doing, without changing how MeshCORE works. No internet routing, no messing with RF, nothing in the message path. It just listens, observes, and feeds back what already happened. Why I built it: • New repeaters often get switched off because it’s hard to tell if they’re helping • People end up spamming test messages just to see if anything’s alive • When stuff doesn’t propagate, there’s no clue why MeshRank gives things like delivery confidence, shows which repeaters are contributing, and highlights where messages are being seen but not going any further. Observers are totally passive — they don’t transmit or interfere in any way. If MeshRank fell over tomorrow, the mesh would carry on exactly the same. I’m looking for a few folks (UK first) who wouldn’t mind running an observer and helping build some early coverage — high ground / urban spots especially useful. If you fancy a look: https://meshrank.net Happy to answer questions or take criticism

messages.author: Mark
messages.text: Having got MT to work here in Devon, we lost a main Node to MC so I started to test MC to see how it compares. The link to Torbay was easy, and I knew I would to have to put out another MC Node to go Northwards to Exeter/Silverton/Exmoor. Silverton was put out in early January followed by E3 in Exmouth. Other Users of MT in Okehampton/Winkleigh/Exeter built MC Nodes to try and replicate what we have in MT. The MT mesh of Nodes took nearly 2 years to build, the MC mesh might be quicker and we have permissions....
messages.content_html: Having got MT to work here in Devon, we lost a main Node to MC so I started to test MC to see how it compares. The MT mesh of Nodes took nearly 2 years to build, the MC mesh might be quicker and we have permissions....

messages.author: Mark
messages.text: Both MT and MC need careful placement of Nodes for them both to work, your personal Node has to see other Nodes in the mesh for it to work properly. MT and MC have their own strengths and weaknesses, overall not one is better than the other, time will only tell.............

messages.author: S7uart G5AIL
messages.text: Morning all — a few updates to MeshRank.net: Heltec V4 can now run as a combined Observer + Repeater (beta). An NTP time sync has been added once Wi-Fi connects, so the device always has the correct date/time after reboot. This also includes the Heltec V4 SX1262 / GC1109 RX fix. As this is beta, please don’t update the repeater that’s bolted to your roof just yet The MQTT feed is now open for anyone already using meshcoretomqtt (or similar) and wanting to mirror their packet stream into MeshRank. Advanced packet routing maps are nearly finished. These are based on mutual neighbour connections and can infer likely paths where there are gaps in the observed route. I’m also finalising MeshLive — a real-time visual view of the mesh, including country-wide noise floor, live routes, and packets per minute. This will be released to observers first.
messages.content_html: Morning all — a few updates to MeshRank.net: Heltec V4 can now run as a combined Observer + Repeater (beta). An NTP time sync has been added once Wi-Fi connects, so the device always has the correct date/time after reboot. This also includes the Heltec V4 SX1262 / GC1109 RX fix. As this is beta, please don’t update the repeater that’s bolted to your roof just yet The MQTT feed is now open for anyone already using meshcoretomqtt (or similar) and wanting to mirror their packet stream into MeshRank. Advanced packet routing maps are nearly finished. These are based on mutual neighbour connections and can infer likely paths where there are gaps in the observed route. I’m also finalising MeshLive — a real-time visual view of the mesh, including country-wide noise floor, live routes, and packets per minute. This will be released to observers first.

messages.author: NS141
messages.text: Afternoon. NS141 here from Exeter, Devon. Had a video pushed to me via the YouTube algorithm about MeshCore. Thought ‘that’s pretty neat’ and went ahead and ordered a used Heltec V3 (awaiting delivery via Vinted/inpost). It currently has meshtastic flashed to it but will flash MeshCore on its arrival. Looking to try it out for now with just the app on my phone before investing further. I’m not too savvy and have limited knowledge, most I’ve ever used is a little handheld running egzumer to listen to local air display shows! More just a heads up really to say that you’re starting to attract those of us that aren’t necessarily part of the scene/hobby but can see how neat of an idea this is (and not be so reliant on regular communication methods). Hopefully figure it out and reach one of you in the area this coming weekend.

Thought ‘that’s pretty neat’ and went ahead and ordered a used Heltec V3 (awaiting delivery via Vinted/inpost). It currently has meshtastic flashed to it but will flash MeshCore on its arrival. Looking to try it out for now with just the app on my phone before investing further. I’m not too savvy and have limited knowledge, most I’ve ever used is a little handheld running egzumer to listen to local air display shows! More just a heads up really to say that you’re starting to attract those of us that aren’t necessarily part of the scene/hobby but can see how neat of an idea this is (and not be so reliant on regular communication methods). Hopefully figure it out and reach one of you in the area this coming weekend.

messages.author: Mark
messages.text: Hello @NS141 I am based in Exmouth and Richard is based in Exwick. At present Exeter dosn't have a lot of MC Users and there is room for more Repeaters. Which part do you live in?
messages.content_html: Hello @NS141 I am based in Exmouth and Richard is based in Exwick. At present Exeter dosn't have a lot of MC Users and there is room for more Repeaters. Which part do you live in?
messages.reply_html:
NS141
Afternoon. NS141 here from Exeter, Devon. Had a video pushed to me via the YouTube algorithm about MeshCore. Thought ‘that’s pretty neat’ and went ahead and ordered a used Heltec V3 (awaiting delivery via Vinted/inpost). It currently has meshtastic flashed to it but will flash MeshCore on its arrival. Looking to try it out for now with just the app on my phone before investing further. I’m not too savvy and have limited knowledge, most I’ve ever used is a little handheld running egzumer to listen to local air display shows! More just a heads up really to say that you’re starting to attract those of us that aren’t necessarily part of the scene/hobby but can see how neat of an idea this is (and not be so reliant on regular communication methods). Hopefully figure it out and reach one of you in the area this coming weekend.

messages.author: NS141
messages.text: Hi Mark. I’m in between Beacon Heath and Pinhoe.
messages.content_html: Hi Mark. I’m in between Beacon Heath and Pinhoe.
messages.reactions_html: 1
messages.reply_html:
Mark
Hello @NS141 I am based in Exmouth and Richard is based in Exwick. At present Exeter dosn't have a lot of MC Users and there is room for more Repeaters. Which part do you live in?

messages.author: Mark
messages.text: Before flashing MC on your Nodes, you could try MT as there is a good network of Nodes in and around Exeter. There is a MT Node high up in Beacon Heathe and another on top of the RDE Hospital
messages.content_html: Before flashing MC on your Nodes, you could try MT as there is a good network of Nodes in and around Exeter. There is a MT Node high up in Beacon Heathe and another on top of the RDE Hospital
messages.reply_html:
NS141
Hi Mark. I’m in between Beacon Heath and Pinhoe.

messages.author: NS141
messages.text: Will give it a try first, thanks for the heads up. Did look at MT and MC maps before purchasing and MC looks to have more going on and more of a push behind it. As I’ve no previous knowledge or affiliation, is it as simple as deciding between Tesco or Sainsbury’s? Maybe it’s best to have both store cards and make use of both… for now?
